curricula.name: The national curriculum in England: Key stages 1 and 2 framework document
curricula.license: Verbatim standard text included under this source’s upstream license. See PROVENANCE.md.

data.title: Use materials creatively
data.subject: Art and design
data.description: to use a range of materials creatively to design and make products

data.title: Drawing, painting and sculpture
data.subject: Art and design
data.description: to use drawing, painting and sculpture to develop and share their ideas, experiences and imagination

data.title: Techniques
data.subject: Art and design
data.description: to develop a wide range of art and design techniques in using colour, pattern, texture, line, shape, form and space

data.title: Artists, craft makers and designers
data.subject: Art and design
data.description: about the work of a range of artists, craft makers and designers, describing the differences and similarities between different practices and disciplines, and making links to their own work.

data.title: Sketch books
data.subject: Art and design
data.description: to create sketch books to record their observations and use them to review and revisit ideas

data.title: Mastery of techniques
data.subject: Art and design
data.description: to improve their mastery of art and design techniques, including drawing, painting and sculpture with a range of materials [for example, pencil, charcoal, paint, clay]

data.title: Great artists, architects and designers
data.subject: Art and design
data.description: about great artists, architects and designers in history.

data.title: Algorithms and programs
data.subject: Computing
data.description: understand what algorithms are; how they are implemented as programs on digital devices; and that programs execute by following precise and unambiguous instructions

data.title: Create and debug
data.subject: Computing
data.description: create and debug simple programs

data.title: Logical reasoning
data.subject: Computing
data.description: use logical reasoning to predict the behaviour of simple programs

data.title: Digital content
data.subject: Computing
data.description: use technology purposefully to create, organise, store, manipulate and retrieve digital content

data.title: IT beyond school
data.subject: Computing
data.description: recognise common uses of information technology beyond school

data.title: Safety and privacy
data.subject: Computing
data.description: use technology safely and respectfully, keeping personal information private; identify where to go for help and support when they have concerns about content or contact on the internet or other online technologies.

data.title: Design, write and debug programs
data.subject: Computing
data.description: design, write and debug programs that accomplish specific goals, including controlling or simulating physical systems; solve problems by decomposing them into smaller parts

data.title: Sequence, selection, and repetition
data.subject: Computing
data.description: use sequence, selection, and repetition in programs; work with variables and various forms of input and output

data.title: Logical reasoning and error detection
data.subject: Computing
data.description: use logical reasoning to explain how some simple algorithms work and to detect and correct errors in algorithms and programs

data.title: Computer networks
data.subject: Computing
data.description: understand computer networks including the internet; how they can provide multiple services, such as the world wide web; and the opportunities they offer for communication and collaboration

data.title: Search technologies
data.subject: Computing
data.description: use search technologies effectively, appreciate how results are selected and ranked, and be discerning in evaluating digital content

data.title: Select and combine software
data.subject: Computing
data.description: select, use and combine a variety of software (including internet services) on a range of digital devices to design and create a range of programs, systems and content that accomplish given goals, including collecting, analysing, evaluating and presenting data and information

data.title: Safety and responsibility
data.subject: Computing
data.description: use technology safely, respectfully and responsibly; recognise acceptable/unacceptable behaviour; identify a range of ways to report concerns about content and contact.

data.title: Healthy diet principles
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: use the basic principles of a healthy and varied diet to prepare dishes

data.title: Source of food
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: understand where food comes from.

data.title: Design purposeful products
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: design purposeful, functional, appealing products for themselves and other users based on design criteria

data.title: Generate and communicate ideas
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: generate, develop, model and communicate their ideas through talking, drawing, templates, mock-ups and, where appropriate, information and communication technology

data.title: Explore existing products
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: explore and evaluate a range of existing products

data.title: Evaluate ideas and products
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: evaluate their ideas and products against design criteria

data.title: Select and use tools
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: select from and use a range of tools and equipment to perform practical tasks [for example, cutting, shaping, joining and finishing]

data.title: Select and use materials
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: select from and use a wide range of materials and components, including construction materials, textiles and ingredients, according to their characteristics

data.title: Build structures
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: build structures, exploring how they can be made stronger, stiffer and more stable

data.title: Explore mechanisms
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: explore and use mechanisms [for example, levers, sliders, wheels and axles], in their products.

data.title: Apply healthy diet principles
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: understand and apply the principles of a healthy and varied diet

data.title: Prepare and cook dishes
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: prepare and cook a variety of predominantly savoury dishes using a range of cooking techniques

data.title: Seasonality and ingredients
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: understand seasonality, and know where and how a variety of ingredients are grown, reared, caught and processed.

data.title: Use research and design criteria
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: use research and develop design criteria to inform the design of innovative, functional, appealing products that are fit for purpose, aimed at particular individuals or groups

data.title: Generate and communicate ideas
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: generate, develop, model and communicate their ideas through discussion, annotated sketches, cross-sectional and exploded diagrams, prototypes, pattern pieces and computer-aided design

data.title: Analyse existing products
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: investigate and analyse a range of existing products

data.title: Evaluate against criteria
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: evaluate their ideas and products against their own design criteria and consider the views of others to improve their work

data.title: Key events and individuals
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: understand how key events and individuals in design and technology have helped shape the world

data.title: Select and use tools accurately
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: select from and use a wider range of tools and equipment to perform practical tasks [for example, cutting, shaping, joining and finishing], accurately

data.title: Select and use materials
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: select from and use a wider range of materials and components, including construction materials, textiles and ingredients, according to their functional properties and aesthetic qualities

data.title: Strengthen structures
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: apply their understanding of how to strengthen, stiffen and reinforce more complex structures

data.title: Mechanical systems
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: understand and use mechanical systems in their products [for example, gears, pulleys, cams, levers and linkages]

data.title: Electrical systems
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: understand and use electrical systems in their products [for example, series circuits incorporating switches, bulbs, buzzers and motors]

data.title: Computing in products
data.subject: Design and technology
data.description: apply their understanding of computing to program, monitor and control their products.

data.description: Evaluate and edit by ensuring the consistent and correct use of tense throughout a piece of writing.

data.title: Ensure agreement and register
data.subject: English
data.description: Evaluate and edit by ensuring correct subject and verb agreement when using singular and plural, distinguishing between the language of speech and writing and choosing the appropriate register.

data.title: Proof-read
data.subject: English
data.description: Evaluate and edit by proof-reading for spelling and punctuation errors.

data.title: Perform compositions
data.subject: English
data.description: Perform their own compositions, using appropriate intonation, volume, and movement so that meaning is clear.

data.title: Choose letter shapes
data.subject: English
data.description: Write legibly, fluently and with increasing speed by choosing which shape of a letter to use when given choices and deciding whether or not to join specific letters.

data.title: Choose writing implement
data.subject: English
data.description: Write legibly, fluently and with increasing speed by choosing the writing implement that is best suited for a task.

data.title: Use prefixes and suffixes
data.subject: English
data.description: Use further prefixes and suffixes and understand the guidance for adding them.

data.title: Spell silent letters
data.subject: English
data.description: Spell some words with ‘silent’ letters [for example, knight, psalm, solemn].

data.title: Distinguish homophones
data.subject: English
data.description: Continue to distinguish between homophones and other words which are often confused.

data.title: Use morphology and etymology
data.subject: English
data.description: Use knowledge of morphology and etymology in spelling and understand that the spelling of some words needs to be learnt specifically, as listed in English Appendix 1.

data.title: Use dictionaries
data.subject: English
data.description: Use dictionaries to check the spelling and meaning of words.

data.title: Use first letters for dictionary check
data.subject: English
data.description: Use the first three or four letters of a word to check spelling, meaning or both of these in a dictionary.

data.title: Use a thesaurus
data.subject: English
data.description: Use a thesaurus.

data.title: Recognise formal vocabulary and structures
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by recognising vocabulary and structures that are appropriate for formal speech and writing, including subjunctive forms.

data.title: Use passive verbs
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by using passive verbs to affect the presentation of information in a sentence.

data.title: Use perfect form of verbs
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by using the perfect form of verbs to mark relationships of time and cause.

data.title: Use expanded noun phrases
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by using expanded noun phrases to convey complicated information concisely.

data.title: Use modal verbs or adverbs
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by using modal verbs or adverbs to indicate degrees of possibility.

data.title: Use relative clauses
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by using relative clauses beginning with who, which, where, when, whose, that or with an implied (i.e. omitted) relative pronoun.

data.title: Learn grammar for years 5 and 6
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by learning the grammar for years 5 and 6 in English Appendix 2.

data.title: Use commas for clarity
data.subject: English
data.description: Indicate grammatical and other features by using commas to clarify meaning or avoid ambiguity in writing.

data.title: Use hyphens
data.subject: English
data.description: Indicate grammatical and other features by using hyphens to avoid ambiguity.

data.title: Indicate parenthesis
data.subject: English
data.description: Indicate grammatical and other features by using brackets, dashes or commas to indicate parenthesis.

data.title: Mark clause boundaries
data.subject: English
data.description: Indicate grammatical and other features by using semi-colons, colons or dashes to mark boundaries between independent clauses.

data.title: Use colon for lists
data.subject: English
data.description: Indicate grammatical and other features by using a colon to introduce a list.

data.title: Punctuating bullet points
data.subject: English
data.description: Indicate grammatical and other features by punctuating bullet points consistently.

data.title: Use grammatical terminology
data.subject: English
data.description: Use and understand the grammatical terminology in English Appendix 2 accurately and appropriately in discussing their writing and reading.

data.title: Listen to poems and stories
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop pleasure in reading, motivation to read, vocabulary and understanding by listening to and discussing a wide range of poems, stories and non-fiction at a level beyond that at which they can read independently.

data.title: Link reading to experience
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop pleasure in reading, motivation to read, vocabulary and understanding by being encouraged to link what they read or hear read to their own experiences.

data.title: Familiarity with key stories
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop pleasure in reading, motivation to read, vocabulary and understanding by becoming very familiar with key stories, fairy stories and traditional tales, retelling them and considering their particular characteristics.

data.title: Recognise predictable phrases
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop pleasure in reading, motivation to read, vocabulary and understanding by recognising and joining in with predictable phrases.

data.title: Appreciate rhymes and poems
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop pleasure in reading, motivation to read, vocabulary and understanding by learning to appreciate rhymes and poems, and to recite some by heart.

data.title: Discuss word meanings
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop pleasure in reading, motivation to read, vocabulary and understanding by discussing word meanings, linking new meanings to those already known.

data.title: Draw on background information
data.subject: English
data.description: Understand both the books they can already read accurately and fluently and those they listen to by drawing on what they already know or on background information and vocabulary provided by the teacher.

data.title: Check sense and correct
data.subject: English
data.description: Understand both the books they can already read accurately and fluently and those they listen to by checking that the text makes sense to them as they read and correcting inaccurate reading.

data.title: Discuss title and events
data.subject: English
data.description: Understand both the books they can already read accurately and fluently and those they listen to by discussing the significance of the title and events.

data.title: Make inferences
data.subject: English
data.description: Understand both the books they can already read accurately and fluently and those they listen to by making inferences on the basis of what is being said and done.

data.title: Predict events
data.subject: English
data.description: Understand both the books they can already read accurately and fluently and those they listen to by predicting what might happen on the basis of what has been read so far.

data.title: Participate in discussion
data.subject: English
data.description: Participate in discussion about what is read to them, taking turns and listening to what others say.

data.title: Explain understanding
data.subject: English
data.description: Explain clearly their understanding of what is read to them.

data.title: Apply phonic knowledge
data.subject: English
data.description: Apply phonic knowledge and skills as the route to decode words.

data.title: Respond to graphemes
data.subject: English
data.description: Respond speedily with the correct sound to graphemes (letters or groups of letters) for all 40+ phonemes, including, where applicable, alternative sounds for graphemes.

data.title: Blend sounds
data.subject: English
data.description: Read accurately by blending sounds in unfamiliar words containing GPCs that have been taught.

data.title: Read common exception words
data.subject: English
data.description: Read common exception words, noting unusual correspondences between spelling and sound and where these occur in the word.

data.title: Read words with endings
data.subject: English
data.description: Read words containing taught GPCs and –s, –es, –ing, –ed, –er and –est endings.

data.title: Read multi-syllable words
data.subject: English
data.description: Read other words of more than one syllable that contain taught GPCs.

data.title: Read contractions
data.subject: English
data.description: Read words with contractions [for example, I’m, I’ll, we’ll], and understand that the apostrophe represents the omitted letter(s).

data.title: Read aloud accurately
data.subject: English
data.description: Read aloud accurately books that are consistent with their developing phonic knowledge and that do not require them to use other strategies to work out words.

data.title: Re-read books
data.subject: English
data.description: Re-read these books to build up their fluency and confidence in word reading.

data.title: Say out loud
data.subject: English
data.description: Write sentences by saying out loud what they are going to write about.

data.title: Compose orally
data.subject: English
data.description: Write sentences by composing a sentence orally before writing it.

data.title: Sequence sentences
data.subject: English
data.description: Write sentences by sequencing sentences to form short narratives.

data.title: Re-read writing
data.subject: English
data.description: Write sentences by re-reading what they have written to check that it makes sense.

data.title: Discuss writing
data.subject: English
data.description: Discuss what they have written with the teacher or other pupils.

data.title: Read aloud writing
data.subject: English
data.description: Read aloud their writing clearly enough to be heard by their peers and the teacher.

data.title: Sit correctly
data.subject: English
data.description: Sit correctly at a table, holding a pencil comfortably and correctly.

data.title: Form lower-case letters
data.subject: English
data.description: Begin to form lower-case letters in the correct direction, starting and finishing in the right place.

data.title: Form capital letters
data.subject: English
data.description: Form capital letters.

data.title: Form digits
data.subject: English
data.description: Form digits 0-9.

data.title: Handwriting families
data.subject: English
data.description: Understand which letters belong to which handwriting ‘families’ (i.e. letters that are formed in similar ways) and to practise these.

data.title: Spell phoneme words
data.subject: English
data.description: Spell words containing each of the 40+ phonemes already taught.

data.title: Spell common exception words
data.subject: English
data.description: Spell common exception words.

data.title: Spell days of the week
data.subject: English
data.description: Spell the days of the week.

data.title: Name letters of the alphabet
data.subject: English
data.description: Name the letters of the alphabet in order.

data.title: Use letter names
data.subject: English
data.description: Use letter names to distinguish between alternative spellings of the same sound.

data.title: Add suffixes -s or -es
data.subject: English
data.description: Add prefixes and suffixes using the spelling rule for adding –s or –es as the plural marker for nouns and the third person singular marker for verbs.

data.title: Add prefix un-
data.subject: English
data.description: Add prefixes and suffixes using the prefix un–.

data.title: Add suffixes -ing, -ed, -er, -est
data.subject: English
data.description: Add prefixes and suffixes using –ing, –ed, –er and –est where no change is needed in the spelling of root words [for example, helping, helped, helper, eating, quicker, quickest].

data.title: Apply spelling rules
data.subject: English
data.description: Apply simple spelling rules and guidance, as listed in English Appendix 1.

data.title: Write dictated sentences
data.subject: English
data.description: Write from memory simple sentences dictated by the teacher that include words using the GPCs and common exception words taught so far.

data.title: Leave spaces
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by leaving spaces between words.

data.title: Join words and clauses
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by joining words and joining clauses using and.

data.title: Punctuating sentences
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by beginning to punctuate sentences using a capital letter and a full stop, question mark or exclamation mark.

data.title: Capital letters for names
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by using a capital letter for names of people, places, the days of the week, and the personal pronoun ‘I’.

data.title: Learn grammar
data.subject: English
data.description: Develop their understanding of the concepts set out in English Appendix 2 by learning the grammar for year 1 in English Appendix 2.

data.title: Use grammatical terminology
data.subject: English
data.description: Use the grammatical terminology in English Appendix 2 in discussing their writing.

data.title: Weather patterns and hot/cold areas
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Identify seasonal and daily weather patterns in the United Kingdom and the location of hot and cold areas of the world in relation to the Equator and the North and South Poles.

data.title: Physical features vocabulary
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use basic geographical vocabulary to refer to: key physical features, including: beach, cliff, coast, forest, hill, mountain, sea, ocean, river, soil, valley, vegetation, season and weather.

data.title: Human features vocabulary
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use basic geographical vocabulary to refer to: key human features, including: city, town, village, factory, farm, house, office, port, harbour and shop.

data.title: Continents and oceans
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Name and locate the world’s seven continents and five oceans.

data.title: UK countries and capitals
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Name, locate and identify characteristics of the four countries and capital cities of the United Kingdom and its surrounding seas.

data.title: Geographical similarities and differences
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Understand geographical similarities and differences through studying the human and physical geography of a small area of the United Kingdom, and of a small area in a contrasting non-European country.

data.title: Using maps, atlases and globes
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use world maps, atlases and globes to identify the United Kingdom and its countries, as well as the countries, continents and oceans studied at this key stage.

data.title: Compass directions and locational language
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use simple compass directions (North, South, East and West) and locational and directional language [for example, near and far; left and right], to describe the location of features and routes on a map.

data.title: Aerial photographs and maps
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use aerial photographs and plan perspectives to recognise landmarks and basic human and physical features; devise a simple map; and use and construct basic symbols in a key.

data.title: Fieldwork and observational skills
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use simple fieldwork and observational skills to study the geography of their school and its grounds and the key human and physical features of its surrounding environment.

data.title: Physical geography aspects
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Describe and understand key aspects of: physical geography, including: climate zones, biomes and vegetation belts, rivers, mountains, volcanoes and earthquakes, and the water cycle.

data.title: Human geography aspects
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Describe and understand key aspects of: human geography, including: types of settlement and land use, economic activity including trade links, and the distribution of natural resources including energy, food, minerals and water.

data.title: Locate world countries
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Locate the world’s countries, using maps to focus on Europe (including the location of Russia) and North and South America, concentrating on their environmental regions, key physical and human characteristics, countries, and major cities.

data.title: UK counties, cities and regions
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Name and locate counties and cities of the United Kingdom, geographical regions and their identifying human and physical characteristics, key topographical features (including hills, mountains, coasts and rivers), and land-use patterns; and understand how some of these aspects have changed over time.

data.title: Latitude, longitude and time zones
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Identify the position and significance of latitude, longitude, Equator, Northern Hemisphere, Southern Hemisphere, the Tropics of Cancer and Capricorn, Arctic and Antarctic Circle, the Prime/Greenwich Meridian and time zones (including day and night).

data.title: Geographical similarities and differences
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Understand geographical similarities and differences through the study of human and physical geography of a region of the United Kingdom, a region in a European country, and a region within North or South America.

data.title: Using maps and digital mapping
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use maps, atlases, globes and digital/computer mapping to locate countries and describe features studied.

data.title: Grid references and symbols
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use the eight points of a compass, four and six-figure grid references, symbols and key (including the use of Ordnance Survey maps) to build their knowledge of the United Kingdom and the wider world.

data.title: Fieldwork techniques
data.subject: Geography
data.description: Use fieldwork to observe, measure, record and present the human and physical features in the local area using a range of methods, including sketch maps, plans and graphs, and digital technologies.

data.title: Changes within living memory
data.subject: History
data.description: Changes within living memory. Where appropriate, these should be used to reveal aspects of change in national life.

data.title: Events beyond living memory
data.subject: History
data.description: Events beyond living memory that are significant nationally or globally [for example, the Great Fire of London, the first aeroplane flight or events commemorated through festivals or anniversaries].

data.title: Significant individuals
data.subject: History
data.description: The lives of significant individuals in the past who have contributed to national and international achievements. Some should be used to compare aspects of life in different periods [for example, Elizabeth I and Queen Victoria, Christopher Columbus and Neil Armstrong, William Caxton and Tim Berners-Lee, Pieter Bruegel the Elder and LS Lowry, Rosa Parks and Emily Davison, Mary Seacole and/or Florence Nightingale and Edith Cavell].

data.title: Local history
data.subject: History
data.description: Significant historical events, people and places in their own locality.

data.title: Stone Age to Iron Age
data.subject: History
data.description: Changes in Britain from the Stone Age to the Iron Age.

data.title: Roman Empire
data.subject: History
data.description: The Roman Empire and its impact on Britain.

data.title: Anglo-Saxons and Scots
data.subject: History
data.description: Britain’s settlement by Anglo-Saxons and Scots.

data.title: Vikings and Anglo-Saxons
data.subject: History
data.description: The Viking and Anglo-Saxon struggle for the Kingdom of England to the time of Edward the Confessor.

data.title: Local history study
data.subject: History
data.description: A local history study.

data.title: British history theme beyond 1066
data.subject: History
data.description: A study of an aspect or theme in British history that extends pupils’ chronological knowledge beyond 1066.

data.title: Earliest civilizations
data.subject: History
data.description: The achievements of the earliest civilizations – an overview of where and when the first civilizations appeared and a depth study of one of the following: Ancient Sumer; The Indus Valley; Ancient Egypt; The Shang Dynasty of Ancient China.

data.title: Ancient Greece
data.subject: History
data.description: Ancient Greece – a study of Greek life and achievements and their influence on the western world.

data.title: Non-European society
data.subject: History
data.description: A non-European society that provides contrasts with British history – one study chosen from: early Islamic civilization, including a study of Baghdad c. AD 900; Mayan civilization c. AD 900; Benin (West Africa) c. AD 900-1300.

data.title: Asking simple questions
data.subject: Science
data.description: asking simple questions and recognising that they can be answered in different ways
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils in years 1 and 2 should explore the world around them and raise their own questions. They should experience different types of scientific enquiries, including practical activities, and begin to recognise ways in which they might answer scientific questions.

data.title: Observing closely
data.subject: Science
data.description: observing closely, using simple equipment
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils in years 1 and 2 should explore the world around them and raise their own questions. They should experience different types of scientific enquiries, including practical activities, and begin to recognise ways in which they might answer scientific questions.

data.title: Performing simple tests
data.subject: Science
data.description: performing simple tests
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils in years 1 and 2 should explore the world around them and raise their own questions. They should experience different types of scientific enquiries, including practical activities, and begin to recognise ways in which they might answer scientific questions.

data.title: Identifying and classifying
data.subject: Science
data.description: identifying and classifying
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils in years 1 and 2 should explore the world around them and raise their own questions. They should experience different types of scientific enquiries, including practical activities, and begin to recognise ways in which they might answer scientific questions.

data.title: Suggesting answers to questions
data.subject: Science
data.description: using their observations and ideas to suggest answers to questions
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils in years 1 and 2 should explore the world around them and raise their own questions. They should experience different types of scientific enquiries, including practical activities, and begin to recognise ways in which they might answer scientific questions.

data.title: Gathering and recording data
data.subject: Science
data.description: gathering and recording data to help in answering questions
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils in years 1 and 2 should explore the world around them and raise their own questions. They should experience different types of scientific enquiries, including practical activities, and begin to recognise ways in which they might answer scientific questions.

data.title: Asking questions and using scientific enquiries
data.subject: Science
data.description: asking relevant questions and using different types of scientific enquiries to answer them
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils in years 3 and 4 should be given a range of scientific experiences to enable them to raise their own questions about the world around them. They should start to make their own decisions about the most appropriate type of scientific enquiry they might use to answer questions; recognise when a simple fair test is necessary and help to decide how to set it up; talk about criteria for grouping, sorting and classifying; and use simple keys. They should begin to look for naturally occurring patterns and relationships and decide what data to collect to identify them.

data.title: Setting up practical enquiries and fair tests
data.subject: Science
data.description: setting up simple practical enquiries, comparative and fair tests
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils should start to make their own decisions about the most appropriate type of scientific enquiry they might use to answer questions; recognise when a simple fair test is necessary and help to decide how to set it up.

data.title: Making observations and taking measurements
data.subject: Science
data.description: making systematic and careful observations and, where appropriate, taking accurate measurements using standard units, using a range of equipment, including thermometers and data loggers
data.notesAndGuidance: They should help to make decisions about what observations to make, how long to make them for and the type of simple equipment that might be used. They should learn how to use new equipment, such as data loggers, appropriately.

data.title: Gathering, recording, classifying and presenting data
data.subject: Science
data.description: gathering, recording, classifying and presenting data in a variety of ways to help in answering questions
data.notesAndGuidance: They should collect data from their own observations and measurements, using notes, simple tables and standard units, and help to make decisions about how to record and analyse this data.

data.title: Recording findings using scientific language and diagrams
data.subject: Science
data.description: recording findings using simple scientific language, drawings, labelled diagrams, keys, bar charts, and tables
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils should use relevant scientific language to discuss their ideas and communicate their findings in ways that are appropriate for different audiences.

data.title: Reporting on findings from enquiries
data.subject: Science
data.description: reporting on findings from enquiries, including oral and written explanations, displays or presentations of results and conclusions
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils should use relevant scientific language to discuss their ideas and communicate their findings in ways that are appropriate for different audiences.

data.title: Drawing conclusions and making predictions
data.subject: Science
data.description: using results to draw simple conclusions, make predictions for new values, suggest improvements and raise further questions
data.notesAndGuidance: With help, pupils should look for changes, patterns, similarities and differences in their data in order to draw simple conclusions and answer questions. With support, they should identify new questions arising from the data, making predictions for new values within or beyond the data they have collected, and finding ways of improving what they have already done.

data.title: Identifying differences, similarities or changes
data.subject: Science
data.description: identifying differences, similarities or changes related to simple scientific ideas and processes
data.notesAndGuidance: With help, pupils should look for changes, patterns, similarities and differences in their data in order to draw simple conclusions and answer questions.

data.title: Using scientific evidence to answer questions
data.subject: Science
data.description: using straightforward scientific evidence to answer questions or to support their findings
data.notesAndGuidance: They should also recognise when and how secondary sources might help them to answer questions that cannot be answered through practical investigations.

data.title: Planning scientific enquiries
data.subject: Science
data.description: planning different types of scientific enquiries to answer questions, including recognising and controlling variables where necessary
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils in years 5 and 6 should use their science experiences to: explore ideas and raise different kinds of questions; select and plan the most appropriate type of scientific enquiry to use to answer scientific questions; recognise when and how to set up comparative and fair tests and explain which variables need to be controlled and why.

data.title: Taking measurements
data.subject: Science
data.description: taking measurements, using a range of scientific equipment, with increasing accuracy and precision, taking repeat readings when appropriate
data.notesAndGuidance: They should make their own decisions about what observations to make, what measurements to use and how long to make them for, and whether to repeat them; choose the most appropriate equipment to make measurements and explain how to use it accurately.

data.title: Recording data and results
data.subject: Science
data.description: recording data and results of increasing complexity using scientific diagrams and labels, classification keys, tables, scatter graphs, bar and line graphs
data.notesAndGuidance: They should decide how to record data from a choice of familiar approaches; look for different causal relationships in their data and identify evidence that refutes or supports their ideas.

data.title: Using test results to make predictions
data.subject: Science
data.description: using test results to make predictions to set up further comparative and fair tests
data.notesAndGuidance: They should use their results to identify when further tests and observations might be needed; recognise which secondary sources will be most useful to research their ideas and begin to separate opinion from fact.

data.title: Reporting and presenting findings
data.subject: Science
data.description: reporting and presenting findings from enquiries, including conclusions, causal relationships and explanations of and a degree of trust in results, in oral and written forms such as displays and other presentations
data.notesAndGuidance: They should use relevant scientific language and illustrations to discuss, communicate and justify their scientific ideas and should talk about how scientific ideas have developed over time.

data.title: Identifying scientific evidence
data.subject: Science
data.description: identifying scientific evidence that has been used to support or refute ideas or arguments
data.notesAndGuidance: They should look for different causal relationships in their data and identify evidence that refutes or supports their ideas. They should recognise which secondary sources will be most useful to research their ideas and begin to separate opinion from fact.

data.title: Extend and apply grammatical knowledge
data.strand: Consolidate and build on their knowledge of grammar and vocabulary
data.description: extending and applying the grammatical knowledge set out in English appendix 2 to the key stage 1 and 2 programmes of study to analyse more challenging texts

data.title: Study effectiveness of grammatical features
data.strand: Consolidate and build on their knowledge of grammar and vocabulary
data.description: studying the effectiveness and impact of the grammatical features of the texts they read

data.title: Draw on new vocabulary and grammatical constructions
data.strand: Consolidate and build on their knowledge of grammar and vocabulary
data.description: drawing on new vocabulary and grammatical constructions from their reading and listening, and using these consciously in their writing and speech to achieve particular effects

data.title: Know differences between spoken and written language
data.strand: Consolidate and build on their knowledge of grammar and vocabulary
data.description: knowing and understanding the differences between spoken and written language, including differences associated with formal and informal registers, and between Standard English and other varieties of English

data.title: Use Standard English confidently
data.strand: Consolidate and build on their knowledge of grammar and vocabulary
data.description: using Standard English confidently in their own writing and speech

data.title: Discuss language with precise terminology
data.strand: Consolidate and build on their knowledge of grammar and vocabulary
data.description: discussing reading, writing and spoken language with precise and confident use of linguistic and literary terminology

data.title: Read a wide range of fiction and non-fiction
data.strand: Develop an appreciation and love of reading, and read increasingly challenging material independently
data.description: reading a wide range of fiction and non-fiction, including in particular whole books, short stories, poems and plays with a wide coverage of genres, historical periods, forms and authors, including high-quality works from English literature, both pre-1914 and contemporary, including prose, poetry and drama; Shakespeare (2 plays) and seminal world literature

data.title: Choose books independently
data.strand: Develop an appreciation and love of reading, and read increasingly challenging material independently
data.description: choosing and reading books independently for challenge, interest and enjoyment

data.title: Reread books to increase familiarity
data.strand: Develop an appreciation and love of reading, and read increasingly challenging material independently
data.description: rereading books encountered earlier to increase familiarity with them and provide a basis for making comparisons

data.title: Learn new vocabulary
data.description: learning new vocabulary, relating it explicitly to known vocabulary and understanding it with the help of context and dictionaries

data.title: Make inferences and refer to evidence
data.description: making inferences and referring to evidence in the text

data.title: Know purpose, audience and context
data.description: knowing the purpose, audience for and context of the writing and drawing on this knowledge to support comprehension

data.title: Check understanding
data.description: checking their understanding to make sure that what they have read makes sense

data.title: Know how language presents meaning
data.description: knowing how language, including figurative language, vocabulary choice, grammar, text structure and organisational features, presents meaning

data.title: Recognise poetic conventions
data.description: recognising a range of poetic conventions and understanding how these have been used

data.title: Study setting, plot, and characterisation
data.description: studying setting, plot, and characterisation, and the effects of these

data.title: Understand dramatists' work and performance
data.description: understanding how the work of dramatists is communicated effectively through performance and how alternative staging allows for different interpretations of a play

data.title: Make critical comparisons
data.description: making critical comparisons across texts

data.title: Study a range of authors
data.description: studying a range of authors, including at least 2 authors in depth each year

data.title: Use Standard English in formal and informal contexts
data.description: using Standard English confidently in a range of formal and informal contexts, including classroom discussion

data.title: Give short speeches and presentations
data.description: giving short speeches and presentations, expressing their own ideas and keeping to the point

data.title: Participate in formal debates and structured discussions

data.title: Summarise and organise material
data.strand: Write accurately, fluently, effectively and at length for pleasure and information
data.description: summarising and organising material, and supporting ideas and arguments with any necessary factual detail

data.description: applying their growing knowledge of vocabulary, grammar and text structure to their writing and selecting the appropriate form

data.description: drawing on knowledge of literary and rhetorical devices from their reading and listening to enhance the impact of their writing

data.description: considering how their writing reflects the audiences and purposes for which it was intended

data.description: amending the vocabulary, grammar and structure of their writing to improve its coherence and overall effectiveness

data.subject: Science

data.subject: Science
data.description: the contribution of Faraday in the development of the generator

data.title: Energy Stores and Systems
data.subject: Science

data.title: Energy Changes in a System
data.subject: Science
data.description: energy changes in a system, allowing the calculation of the amount of energy stored, released or dissipated

data.title: Energy Efficiency and Work Done
data.subject: Science

data.subject: Science
data.description: power as the rate of transfer of energy; comparing energy transfers in different contexts

data.title: Heat Transfer and Temperature
data.subject: Science
data.description: the dependence of the rate of heat transfer on the temperature difference

data.title: Simple Machines
data.subject: Science

data.title: Measuring Energy Transfers
data.subject: Science
data.description: measuring and calculating energy transfers in heating experiments

data.title: Speed, Distance and Time
data.subject: Science
data.description: speed and the quantitative relationship between average speed, distance and time (speed = distance ÷ time)

data.title: Gravity, Weight and Mass
data.subject: Science
data.description: gravity force, weight = mass × gravitational field strength (g), on Earth g = 10 N/kg, different on other planets and stars

data.title: Distance-Time Graphs
data.subject: Science
data.description: the representation of a journey on a distance-time graph

data.title: Relative Motion
data.subject: Science
data.description: relative motion: trains and cars passing one another

data.title: Forces as Vectors
data.subject: Science
data.description: forces as vectors; balanced and unbalanced forces; a stationary object and forces acting on it

data.title: Deformation, Thermal Expansion and Fluid Pressure
data.subject: Science
data.description: forces: associated with deforming objects; thermal expansion and contraction of matter; fluid pressure

data.title: Turning Effect and Pressure
data.subject: Science
data.description: the turning effect of forces; pressure in liquids transmitting pressure equally in all directions; atmospheric pressure

data.title: Newton's Laws of Motion
data.subject: Science
data.description: forces and Newton's Laws: Newton's First Law; forces and acceleration (Newton's Second Law); Newton's Third Law

data.title: Magnetic Poles and Fields
data.subject: Science
data.description: magnetic poles, attraction and repulsion; magnetic fields by plotting with compass; the Earth's magnetic field

data.title: Elastic Potential Energy and Hooke's Law
data.subject: Science
data.description: elastic potential energy stored in a spring; the elastic limit; Hooke's Law; restoring force

data.title: Gravity and Orbital Motion
data.subject: Science
data.description: gravity force, weight = mass × gravitational field strength (g); orbital motion; weightlessness

data.title: Stars and Galaxies
data.subject: Science
data.description: our Sun as a star, other stars in our galaxy, other galaxies; the Sun as the power source for the solar system

data.title: Seasons and Lunar Phases
data.subject: Science
data.description: the seasons and the Earth's tilt; the distance from the Earth varying the energy received; the phases of the Moon

data.title: The Solar System
data.subject: Science
data.description: the Sun, Earth and Moon as approximately spherical bodies; the relative sizes and distances of the Sun, Earth, Moon and solar system

data.title: Astronomical Distances and Star Life Cycles
data.subject: Science
data.description: the light year as a unit of astronomical distance; using light years to describe distances to stars and galaxies; the life cycle of a star (qualitative)

data.title: The Big Bang Theory
data.subject: Science
data.description: the Big Bang theory and the age and expansion of the universe; red-shift provides evidence for this

data.title: Properties of Waves
data.subject: Science

data.title: Wave Speed, Frequency and Wavelength
data.subject: Science
data.description: the dependence of wave speed on the medium; relationship between wave speed, frequency and wavelength (wave speed = frequency × wavelength)

data.title: Sound Waves
data.subject: Science

data.subject: Science

data.title: Visible Light and the Spectrum

data.title: Apply mathematical concepts

data.description: use SI units and IUPAC chemical nomenclature unless inappropriate

data.description: carry out experiments appropriately having identified potential risks

data.title: Collect and record data
data.description: collect and record data and results of sufficient quality to enable analysis

data.title: Evaluate methods and suggest improvements
data.description: evaluate methods and suggest possible improvements and further investigations

data.title: Develop scientific vocabulary
data.description: develop their use of scientific vocabulary and nomenclature

data.title: Recognise importance of scientific quantities (vocab)
data.description: recognise the importance of scientific quantities and understand how to use them

data.title: Use SI units and IUPAC nomenclature (vocab)
data.description: use SI units and IUPAC chemical nomenclature unless inappropriate

data.title: Interconvert units
data.description: interconvert units

data.title: Use significant figures
data.description: use an appropriate number of significant figures in calculations and conclusions

data.title: Listening and responding
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Listen attentively to spoken language and show understanding by joining in and responding.

data.title: Writing phrases and sentences
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Write phrases from memory, and adapt these to create new sentences, to express ideas clearly.

data.title: Describing people and places
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Describe people, places, things and actions orally and in writing.

data.title: Basic grammar
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Understand basic grammar appropriate to the language being studied, including (where relevant): feminine, masculine and neuter forms and the conjugation of high-frequency verbs; key features and patterns of the language; how to apply these, for instance, to build sentences; and how these differ from or are similar to English.

data.title: Patterns and sounds
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Explore the patterns and sounds of language through songs and rhymes and link the spelling, sound and meaning of words.

data.subject: Languages
data.description: Engage in conversations; ask and answer questions; express opinions and respond to those of others; seek clarification and help.

data.title: Speaking in sentences
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Speak in sentences, using familiar vocabulary, phrases and basic language structures.

data.title: Pronunciation and intonation
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Develop accurate pronunciation and intonation so that others understand when they are reading aloud or using familiar words and phrases.

data.title: Presenting ideas
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Present ideas and information orally to a range of audiences.

data.title: Reading comprehension
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Read carefully and show understanding of words, phrases and simple writing.

data.subject: Languages
data.description: Appreciate stories, songs, poems and rhymes in the language.

data.title: Vocabulary development
data.subject: Languages
data.description: Broaden their vocabulary and develop their ability to understand new words that are introduced into familiar written material, including through using a dictionary.

data.title: Read, write, order and compare numbers to 1,000,000

data.description: Count forwards or backwards in steps of powers of 10 for any given number up to 1 000 000.

data.description: Interpret negative numbers in context, count forwards and backwards with positive and negative whole numbers, including through zero.

data.title: Round numbers to 1,000,000
data.subject: Mathematics
data.description: Round any number up to 1 000 000 to the nearest 10, 100, 1000, 10 000 and 100 000.

data.subject: Mathematics
data.description: Solve number problems and practical problems that involve all of the above.

data.subject: Mathematics
data.description: Read Roman numerals to 1000 (M) and recognise years written in Roman numerals.

data.description: Add and subtract whole numbers with more than 4 digits, including using formal written methods (columnar addition and subtraction).

data.subject: Mathematics
data.description: Add and subtract numbers mentally with increasingly large numbers.

data.title: Use rounding to check answers

data.description: Use rounding to check answers to calculations and determine, in the context of a problem, levels of accuracy.

data.description: Multiply and divide whole numbers and those involving decimals by 10, 100 and 1000.

data.subject: Mathematics

data.subject: Mathematics
data.description: Solve number and practical problems that involve all of the above.

data.title: Identify and name common animals
data.subject: Science
data.description: identify and name a variety of common animals including fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils should use the local environment throughout the year to explore and answer questions about animals in their habitat. They should understand how to take care of animals taken from their local environment and the need to return them safely after study. Pupils should become familiar with the common names of some fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals, including those that are kept as pets. Pupils should have plenty of opportunities to learn the names of the main body parts (including head, neck, arms, elbows, legs, knees, face, ears, eyes, hair, mouth, teeth) through games, actions, songs and rhymes.
data.workingScientifically: Pupils might work scientifically by: using their observations to compare and contrast animals at first hand or through videos and photographs, describing how they identify and group them; grouping animals according to what they eat; and using their senses to compare different textures, sounds and smells.

They should understand how to take care of animals taken from their local environment and the need to return them safely after study. Pupils should become familiar with the common names of some fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals, including those that are kept as pets.
data.workingScientifically: Pupils might work scientifically by: using their observations to compare and contrast animals at first hand or through videos and photographs, describing how they identify and group them; grouping animals according to what they eat; and using their senses to compare different textures, sounds and smells.

data.workingScientifically: Pupils might work scientifically by: using their observations to compare and contrast animals at first hand or through videos and photographs, describing how they identify and group them; grouping animals according to what they eat; and using their senses to compare different textures, sounds and smells.

data.title: Distinguish objects from materials
data.subject: Science
data.description: distinguish between an object and the material from which it is made
data.notesAndGuidance: Pupils should explore, name, discuss and raise and answer questions about everyday materials so that they become familiar with the names of materials and properties such as: hard/soft; stretchy/stiff; shiny/dull; rough/smooth; bendy/not bendy; waterproof/not waterproof; absorbent/not absorbent; opaque/transparent.
data.workingScientifically: Pupils might work scientifically by: performing simple tests to explore questions, for example: 'What is the best material for an umbrella? for lining a dog basket? for curtains? for a bookshelf? for a gymnast's leotard?'

data.workingScientifically: Pupils might work scientifically by: performing simple tests to explore questions, for example: 'What is the best material for an umbrella? for lining a dog basket? for curtains? for a bookshelf? for a gymnast's leotard?'
