data.title: CCRA.R.1
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.

data.title: CCRA.R.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.

data.title: CCRA.R.2
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.

data.title: CCRA.R.3
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.

data.title: CCRA.R.4
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.

data.title: CCRA.R.5
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.

data.title: CCRA.R.6
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.

data.title: CCRA.R.7
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse formats and media, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.

data.title: CCRA.R.8
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.

data.title: CCRA.R.9
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.subStrand: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards
data.description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.

data.title: CCRA.SL.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

data.title: CCRA.SL.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

data.title: CCRA.SL.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Evaluate a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.

data.title: CCRA.SL.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

data.title: CCRA.SL.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.

data.title: CCRA.SL.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.

data.title: CCRA.W.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.

data.title: CCRA.W.10
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

data.title: CCRA.W.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.

data.title: CCRA.W.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.

data.title: CCRA.W.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

data.title: CCRA.W.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.

data.title: CCRA.W.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.

data.title: CCRA.W.7
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.

data.title: CCRA.W.8
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.

data.title: CCRA.W.9
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.

data.title: CCRA.SL.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

data.title: CCRA.SL.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

data.title: CCRA.SL.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Evaluate a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.

data.title: CCRA.SL.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

data.title: CCRA.SL.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.

data.title: CCRA.SL.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.

data.title: SL.11-12.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grades 11–12 topics, texts, and issues, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

data.title: SL.11-12.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.

data.title: SL.11-12.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Work with peers to promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making, set clear goals and deadlines, and establish individual roles as needed.

data.title: SL.11-12.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that probe reasoning and evidence; ensure a hearing for a full range of positions on a topic or issue; clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions; and promote divergent and creative perspectives.

data.title: SL.11-12.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives; synthesize comments, claims, and evidence made on all sides of an issue; resolve contradictions when possible; and determine what additional information or research is required to deepen the investigation or complete the task.

data.title: SL.11-12.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) in order to make informed decisions and solve problems, evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source and noting any discrepancies among the data.

data.title: SL.11-12.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Evaluate a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, assessing the stance, premises, links among ideas, word choice, points of emphasis, and tone used.

data.title: SL.11-12.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence, conveying a clear and distinct perspective, such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning, alternative or opposing perspectives are addressed, and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and a range of formal and informal tasks.

data.title: SL.11-12.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.

data.title: SL.11-12.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating a command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grades 11–12 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 54 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.6.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.

data.title: SL.6.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.

data.title: SL.6.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.

data.title: SL.6.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.

data.title: SL.6.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.

data.title: SL.6.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.

data.title: SL.6.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Delineate a speaker’s argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.

data.title: SL.6.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.

data.title: SL.6.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.

data.title: SL.6.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 6 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 53 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.7.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.

data.title: SL.7.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.

data.title: SL.7.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.

data.title: SL.7.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others’ questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.

data.title: SL.7.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.

data.title: SL.7.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.

data.title: SL.7.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Delineate a speaker’s argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.

data.title: SL.7.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.

data.title: SL.7.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.

data.title: SL.7.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 7 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 53 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.8.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.

data.title: SL.8.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.

data.title: SL.8.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.

data.title: SL.8.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others’ questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.

data.title: SL.8.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.

data.title: SL.8.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.

data.title: SL.8.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Delineate a speaker’s argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.

data.title: SL.8.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.

data.title: SL.8.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.

data.title: SL.8.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 8 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 53 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.9-10.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grades 9–10 topics, texts, and issues, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

data.title: SL.9-10.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.

data.title: SL.9-10.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making (e.g., informal consensus, taking votes on key issues, presentation of alternate views), clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.

data.title: SL.9-10.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions.

data.title: SL.9-10.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views and understanding and make new connections in light of the evidence and reasoning presented.

data.title: SL.9-10.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source.

data.title: SL.9-10.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Evaluate a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, identifying any fallacious reasoning or exaggerated or distorted evidence.

data.title: SL.9-10.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence clearly, concisely, and logically such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and task.

data.title: SL.9-10.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.

data.title: SL.9-10.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grades 9–10 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 54 for specific expectations.)

data.title: L.1.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.11-12.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.11-12.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.

data.title: L.11-12.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Resolve issues of complex or contested usage, consulting references (e.g., Merriam-Webster’s Dictionary of English Usage, Garner’s Modern American Usage) as needed.

data.title: L.11-12.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.11-12.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Observe hyphenation conventions.

data.title: L.11-12.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Spell correctly.

data.title: L.11-12.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

data.title: L.11-12.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references (e.g., Tufte’s Artful Sentences) for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.

data.title: L.11-12.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11–12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

data.title: L.11-12.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word’s position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

data.title: L.11-12.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., conceive, conception, conceivable).

data.title: L.11-12.4c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.

data.title: L.11-12.4d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).

data.title: L.11-12.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.11-12.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.

data.title: L.11-12.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.

data.title: L.11-12.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

data.title: L.1.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.

data.title: L.1.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.

data.title: L.1.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops; We hop).

data.title: L.1.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their; anyone, everything).

data.title: L.1.1e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).

data.title: L.1.1f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.

data.title: L.1.1g
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).

data.title: L.1.1h
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use determiners (e.g., articles, demonstratives).

data.title: L.1.1i
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., during, beyond, toward).

data.title: L.1.1j
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.

data.title: L.1.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.1.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Capitalize dates and names of people.

data.title: L.1.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use end punctuation for sentences.

data.title: L.1.2c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.

data.title: L.1.2d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.

data.title: L.1.2e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.

data.title: L.1.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.

data.title: L.1.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

data.title: L.1.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.

data.title: L.1.4c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).

data.title: L.1.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.1.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Sort words into categories (e.g., colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.

data.title: L.1.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).

data.title: L.1.5c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).

data.title: L.1.5d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.

data.title: L.1.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., because).

data.title: L.2.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.2.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group).

data.title: L.2.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).

data.title: L.2.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).

data.title: L.2.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).

data.title: L.2.1e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.

data.title: L.2.1f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (e.g., The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).

data.title: L.2.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.2.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.

data.title: L.2.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.

data.title: L.2.2c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.

data.title: L.2.2d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).

data.title: L.2.2e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.

data.title: L.2.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

data.title: L.2.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.

data.title: L.2.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.

data.title: L.2.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

data.title: L.2.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).

data.title: L.2.4c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).

data.title: L.2.4d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).

data.title: L.2.4e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.

data.title: L.2.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.2.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).

data.title: L.2.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).

data.title: L.2.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe (e.g., When other kids are happy that makes me happy).

data.title: L.3.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.3.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.

data.title: L.3.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.

data.title: L.3.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).

data.title: L.3.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.

data.title: L.3.1e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use the simple (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.

data.title: L.3.1f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.

data.title: L.3.1g
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.

data.title: L.3.1h
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.

data.title: L.3.1i
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.

data.title: L.3.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.3.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.

data.title: L.3.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use commas in addresses.

data.title: L.3.2c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.

data.title: L.3.2d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use possessives.

data.title: L.3.2e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).

data.title: L.3.2f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.

data.title: L.3.2g
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.

data.title: L.3.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

data.title: L.3.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Choose words and phrases for effect.

data.title: L.3.3b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.

data.title: L.3.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

data.title: L.3.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

data.title: L.3.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).

data.title: L.3.4c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).

data.title: L.3.4d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.

data.title: L.3.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.3.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).

data.title: L.3.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).

data.title: L.3.5c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).

data.title: L.3.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., After dinner that night we went looking for them).

data.title: L.4.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.4.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use relative pronouns (who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (where, when, why).

data.title: L.4.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use the progressive (e.g., I was walking; I am walking; I will be walking) verb tenses.

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use modal auxiliaries (e.g., can, may, must) to convey various conditions.

data.title: L.4.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (e.g., a small red bag rather than a red small bag).

data.title: L.4.1e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use prepositional phrases.

data.title: L.4.1f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.

data.title: L.4.1g
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their).

data.title: L.4.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.4.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use correct capitalization.

data.title: L.4.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.

data.title: L.4.2c
data.subject: English Language Arts

data.title: L.4.2d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.

data.title: L.4.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

data.title: L.4.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.

data.title: L.4.3b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Choose punctuation for effect.

data.title: L.4.3c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion).

data.subject: English Language Arts

data.description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.4.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.

data.title: L.4.5c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).

data.description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (e.g., quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (e.g., wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).

data.subject: English Language Arts

data.title: L.5.1b

data.subject: English Language Arts

data.title: L.5.1e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use correlative conjunctions (e.g., either/or, neither/nor).

data.subject: English Language Arts

data.subject: English Language Arts

data.title: L.6.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.6.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).

data.title: L.6.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).

data.title: L.6.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.

data.title: L.6.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).

data.title: L.6.1e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others’ writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.

data.title: L.6.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.6.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.

data.title: L.6.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Spell correctly.

data.title: L.6.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

data.title: L.6.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.

data.title: L.6.3b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.

data.title: L.6.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

data.title: L.6.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word’s position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

data.title: L.6.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible).

data.title: L.6.4c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.

data.title: L.6.4d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).

data.title: L.6.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.6.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., personification) in context.

data.title: L.6.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.

data.title: L.6.5c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, thrifty).

data.title: L.6.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

data.title: L.7.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.7.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.

data.title: L.7.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.

data.title: L.7.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.

data.title: L.7.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.7.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie but not He wore an old[,] green shirt).

data.title: L.7.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Spell correctly.

data.title: L.7.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

data.title: L.7.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.

data.title: L.7.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

data.title: L.7.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word’s position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

data.title: L.7.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).

data.title: L.7.4c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.

data.title: L.7.4d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).

data.title: L.7.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.7.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, and mythological allusions) in context.

data.title: L.7.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.

data.title: L.7.5c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).

data.title: L.7.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

data.title: L.8.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.8.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain the function of verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences.

data.title: L.8.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.

data.title: L.8.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive mood.

data.title: L.8.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood.

data.title: L.8.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.8.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.

data.title: L.8.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.

data.title: L.8.2c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Spell correctly.

data.title: L.8.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

data.title: L.8.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects (e.g., emphasizing the actor or the action; expressing uncertainty or describing a state contrary to fact).

data.title: L.8.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

data.title: L.8.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word’s position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

data.title: L.8.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, secede).

data.title: L.8.4c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.

data.title: L.8.4d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).

data.title: L.8.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.8.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g. verbal irony, puns) in context.

data.title: L.8.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.

data.title: L.8.5c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., bullheaded, willful, firm, persistent, resolute).

data.title: L.8.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

data.title: L.9-10.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.9-10.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use parallel structure.

data.title: L.9-10.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use various types of phrases (noun, verb, adjectival, adverbial, participial, prepositional, absolute) and clauses (independent, dependent; noun, relative, adverbial) to convey specific meanings and add variety and interest to writing or presentations.

data.title: L.9-10.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.9-10.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use a semicolon (and perhaps a conjunctive adverb) to link two or more closely related independent clauses.

data.title: L.9-10.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use a colon to introduce a list or quotation.

data.title: L.9-10.2c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Spell correctly.

data.title: L.9-10.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

data.title: L.9-10.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write and edit work so that it conforms to the guidelines in a style manual (e.g., MLA Handbook, Turabian’s Manual for Writers) appropriate for the discipline and writing type.

data.title: L.9-10.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 9–10 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

data.title: L.9-10.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word’s position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

data.title: L.9-10.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., analyze, analysis, analytical; advocate, advocacy).

data.title: L.9-10.4c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, or its etymology.

data.title: L.9-10.4d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).

data.title: L.9-10.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.9-10.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., euphemism, oxymoron) in context and analyze their role in the text.

data.title: L.9-10.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.

data.title: L.9-10.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

data.title: L.CCR.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.CCR.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.CCR.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

data.title: L.CCR.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.

data.title: L.CCR.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.CCR.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.

data.title: L.K.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

data.title: L.K.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.

data.title: L.K.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.

data.title: L.K.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).

data.title: L.K.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).

data.title: L.K.1e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).

data.title: L.K.1f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.

data.title: L.K.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

data.title: L.K.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.

data.title: L.K.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and name end punctuation.

data.title: L.K.2c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).

data.title: L.K.2d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.

data.title: L.K.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.

data.title: L.K.4a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).

data.title: L.K.4b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.

data.title: L.K.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.

data.title: L.K.5a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.

data.title: L.K.5b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).

data.title: L.K.5c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).

data.title: L.K.5d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.

data.title: L.K.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.

data.title: RF.1.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.

data.title: RF.1.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).

data.title: RF.1.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).

data.title: RF.1.2.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.

data.title: RF.1.2.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.

data.title: RF.1.2.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.

data.title: RF.1.2.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).

data.title: RF.1.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.

data.title: RF.1.3.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.

data.title: RF.1.3.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.

data.title: RF.1.3.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.

data.title: RF.1.3.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.

data.title: RF.1.3.e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.

data.title: RF.1.3.f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read words with inflectional endings.

data.title: RF.1.3.g
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.

data.title: RF.1.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.

data.title: RF.1.4.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.

data.title: RF.1.4.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.

data.title: RF.1.4.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.

data.title: RF.2.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.

data.title: RF.2.3.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.

data.title: RF.2.3.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.

data.title: RF.2.3.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.

data.title: RF.2.3.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.

data.title: RF.2.3.e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.

data.title: RF.2.3.f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.

data.title: RF.2.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.

data.title: RF.2.4.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.

data.title: RF.2.4.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.

data.title: RF.2.4.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.

data.title: RF.3.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.

data.title: RF.3.3.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.

data.title: RF.3.3.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.

data.title: RF.3.3.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Decode multisyllable words.

data.title: RF.3.3.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.

data.title: RF.3.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.

data.title: RF.3.4.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.

data.title: RF.3.4.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.

data.title: RF.3.4.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.

data.title: RF.4.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.

data.title: RF.4.3.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.

data.title: RF.4.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.

data.title: RF.4.4.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.

data.title: RF.4.4.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.

data.title: RF.4.4.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.

data.title: RF.5.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.

data.title: RF.5.3.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.

data.title: RF.5.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.

data.title: RF.5.4.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.

data.title: RF.5.4.c
