data.title: RF.K.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.

data.title: RF.K.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

data.title: RF.K.1.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.

data.title: RF.K.1.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.

data.title: RF.K.1.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

data.title: RF.K.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).

data.title: RF.K.2.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.

data.title: RF.K.2.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.

data.title: RF.K.2.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.

data.title: RF.K.2.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) words. (This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)

data.title: RF.K.2.e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.

data.title: RF.K.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.

data.title: RF.K.3.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary sound or many of the most frequent sounds for each consonant.

data.title: RF.K.3.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Associate the long and short sounds with common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels.

data.title: RF.K.3.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read common high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).

data.title: RF.K.3.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.

data.title: RF.K.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Read emergent-reader texts with purpose and understanding.

data.title: RH.11-12.1
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, connecting insights gained from specific details to an understanding of the text as a whole.

data.title: RH.11-12.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 11–CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RH.11-12.2
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary that makes clear the relationships among the key details and ideas.

data.title: RH.11-12.3
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Evaluate various explanations for actions or events and determine which explanation best accords with textual evidence, acknowledging where the text leaves matters uncertain.

data.title: RH.11-12.4
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including analyzing how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term over the course of a text (e.g., how Madison defines faction in Federalist No. 10).

data.title: RH.11-12.5
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze in detail how a complex primary source is structured, including how key sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text contribute to the whole.

data.title: RH.11-12.6
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Evaluate authors’ differing points of view on the same historical event or issue by assessing the authors’ claims, reasoning, and evidence.

data.title: RH.11-12.7
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., visually, quantitatively, as well as in words) in order to address a question or solve a problem.

data.title: RH.11-12.8
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Evaluate an author’s premises, claims, and evidence by corroborating or challenging them with other information.

data.title: RH.11-12.9
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Integrate information from diverse sources, both primary and secondary, into a coherent understanding of an idea or event, noting discrepancies among sources.

data.title: RH.6-8.1
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.

data.title: RH.6-8.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RH.6-8.2
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.

data.title: RH.6-8.3
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Identify key steps in a text’s description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).

data.title: RH.6-8.4
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.

data.title: RH.6-8.5
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally).

data.title: RH.6-8.6
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author’s point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).

data.title: RH.6-8.7
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.

data.title: RH.6-8.8
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.

data.title: RH.6-8.9
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.

data.title: RH.9-10.1
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, attending to such features as the date and origin of the information.

data.title: RH.9-10.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 9–10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RH.9-10.2
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of how key events or ideas develop over the course of the text.

data.title: RH.9-10.3
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze in detail a series of events described in a text; determine whether earlier events caused later ones or simply preceded them.

data.title: RH.9-10.4
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary describing political, social, or economic aspects of history/social studies.

data.title: RH.9-10.5
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze how a text uses structure to emphasize key points or advance an explanation or analysis.

data.title: RH.9-10.6
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Compare the point of view of two or more authors for how they treat the same or similar topics, including which details they include and emphasize in their respective accounts.

data.title: RH.9-10.7
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Integrate quantitative or technical analysis (e.g., charts, research data) with qualitative analysis in print or digital text.

data.title: RH.9-10.8
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Assess the extent to which the reasoning and evidence in a text support the author’s claims.

data.title: RH.9-10.9
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Compare and contrast treatments of the same topic in several primary and secondary sources.

data.title: RI.1.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.

data.title: RI.1.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, read informational texts appropriately complex for grade 1.

data.title: RI.11-12.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

data.title: RI.11-12.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 11–CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range. By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 11–CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RI.11-12.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine two or more central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to provide a complex analysis; provide an objective summary of the text.

data.title: RI.11-12.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze a complex set of ideas or sequence of events and explain how specific individuals, ideas, or events interact and develop over the course of the text.

data.title: RI.11-12.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term or terms over the course of a text (e.g., how Madison defines faction in Federalist No. 10).

data.title: RI.11-12.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze and evaluate the effectiveness of the structure an author uses in his or her exposition or argument, including whether the structure makes points clear, convincing, and engaging.

data.title: RI.11-12.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine an author’s point of view or purpose in a text in which the rhetoric is particularly effective, analyzing how style and content contribute to the power, persuasiveness, or beauty of the text.

data.title: RI.11-12.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words in order to address a question or solve a problem.

data.title: RI.11-12.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U.S. texts, including the application of constitutional principles and use of legal reasoning (e.g., in U.S. Supreme Court majority opinions and dissents) and the premises, purposes, and arguments in works of public advocacy (e.g., The Federalist, presidential addresses).

data.title: RI.11-12.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century foundational U.S. documents of historical and literary significance (including The Declaration of Independence, the Preamble to the Constitution, the Bill of Rights, and Lincoln’s Second Inaugural Address) for their themes, purposes, and rhetorical features.

data.title: RI.1.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.

data.title: RI.1.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.

data.title: RI.1.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.

data.title: RI.1.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know and use various text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.

data.title: RI.1.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.

data.title: RI.1.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.

data.title: RI.1.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.

data.title: RI.1.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).

data.title: RI.2.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.

data.title: RI.2.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 2–3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

data.title: RI.2.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify the main topic of a multiparagraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.

data.title: RI.2.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.

data.title: RI.2.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.

data.title: RI.2.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.

data.title: RI.2.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.

data.title: RI.2.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.

data.title: RI.2.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.

data.title: RI.2.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.

data.title: RI.3.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.

data.title: RI.3.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 2–3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RI.3.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.

data.title: RI.3.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.

data.title: RI.3.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.

data.title: RI.3.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.

data.title: RI.3.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.

data.title: RI.3.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur).

data.title: RI.3.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).

data.title: RI.3.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.

data.title: RI.4.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.

data.title: RI.4.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 4–5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

data.title: RI.4.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.

data.title: RI.4.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.

data.title: RI.4.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.

data.title: RI.4.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.

data.title: RI.4.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.

data.title: RI.4.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.

data.title: RI.4.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.

data.title: RI.4.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.

data.title: RI.5.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.

data.title: RI.5.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 4–5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RI.5.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.

data.title: RI.5.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text based on specific information in the text.

data.title: RI.5.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.

data.title: RI.5.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts.

data.title: RI.5.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.

data.title: RI.5.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.

data.title: RI.5.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).

data.title: RI.5.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.

data.title: RI.6.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

data.title: RI.6.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

data.title: RI.6.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.

data.title: RI.6.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).

data.title: RI.6.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.

data.title: RI.6.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.

data.title: RI.6.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine an author’s point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.

data.title: RI.6.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.

data.title: RI.6.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.

data.title: RI.6.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast one author’s presentation of events with that of another (e.g., a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).

data.title: RI.7.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

data.title: RI.7.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

data.title: RI.7.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.

data.title: RI.7.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).

data.title: RI.7.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.

data.title: RI.7.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.

data.title: RI.7.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine an author’s point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.

data.title: RI.7.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium’s portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).

data.title: RI.7.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.

data.title: RI.7.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.

data.title: RI.8.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

data.title: RI.8.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RI.8.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.

data.title: RI.8.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies, or categories).

data.title: RI.8.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.

data.title: RI.8.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.

data.title: RI.8.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine an author’s point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.

data.title: RI.8.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e.g., print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.

data.title: RI.8.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.

data.title: RI.8.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.

data.title: RI.9-10.1

data.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 9–10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range. By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 9–10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RI.9-10.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.

data.title: RI.9-10.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how the author unfolds an analysis or series of ideas or events, including the order in which the points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections that are drawn between them.

data.title: RI.9-10.4
