data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language of a court opinion differs from that of a newspaper).

data.title: RI.9-10.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze in detail how an author’s ideas or claims are developed and refined by particular sentences, paragraphs, or larger portions of a text (e.g., a section or chapter).

data.title: RI.9-10.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine an author’s point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how an author uses rhetoric to advance that point of view or purpose.

data.title: RI.9-10.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze various accounts of a subject told in different mediums (e.g., a person’s life story in both print and multimedia), determining which details are emphasized in each account.

data.title: RI.9-10.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning.

data.title: RI.9-10.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze seminal U.S. documents of historical and literary significance (e.g., Washington’s Farewell Address, the Gettysburg Address, Roosevelt’s Four Freedoms speech, King’s “Letter from Birmingham Jail”), including how they address related themes and concepts.

data.title: RI.K.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.

data.title: RI.K.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.

data.title: RI.K.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.

data.title: RI.K.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.

data.title: RI.K.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.

data.title: RI.K.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

data.title: RI.K.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Name the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in a text.

data.title: RI.K.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in which they appear (e.g., what person, place, thing, or idea in the text an illustration depicts).

data.title: RI.K.8
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.

data.title: RI.K.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).

data.title: RL.1.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.

data.title: RL.1.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, read prose and poetry of appropriate complexity for grade 1.

data.title: RL.11-12.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

data.title: RL.11-12.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 11–CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range. By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 11–CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RL.11-12.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text.

data.title: RL.11-12.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze the impact of the author’s choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters are introduced and developed).

data.title: RL.11-12.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language that is particularly fresh, engaging, or beautiful. (Include Shakespeare as well as other authors.)

data.title: RL.11-12.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how an author’s choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.

data.title: RL.11-12.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze a case in which grasping point of view requires distinguishing what is directly stated in a text from what is really meant (e.g., satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement).

data.title: RL.11-12.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem (e.g., recorded or live production of a play or recorded novel or poetry), evaluating how each version interprets the source text. (Include at least one play by Shakespeare and one play by an American dramatist.)

data.title: RL.11-12.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics.

data.title: RL.1.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.

data.title: RL.1.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.

data.title: RL.1.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.

data.title: RL.1.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information, drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.

data.title: RL.1.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.

data.title: RL.1.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.

data.title: RL.1.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.

data.title: RL.2.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.

data.title: RL.2.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories and poetry, in the grades 2–3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

data.title: RL.2.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.

data.title: RL.2.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.

data.title: RL.2.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe how words and phrases (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.

data.title: RL.2.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.

data.title: RL.2.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.

data.title: RL.2.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.

data.title: RL.2.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.

data.title: RL.3.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.

data.title: RL.3.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 2–3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RL.3.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.

data.title: RL.3.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.

data.title: RL.3.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.

data.title: RL.3.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.

data.title: RL.3.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.

data.title: RL.3.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain how specific aspects of a text’s illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).

data.title: RL.3.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).

data.title: RL.4.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.

data.title: RL.4.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in the grades 4–5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

data.title: RL.4.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.

data.title: RL.4.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character’s thoughts, words, or actions).

data.title: RL.4.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in mythology (e.g., Herculean).

data.title: RL.4.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse, rhythm, meter) and drama (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.

data.title: RL.4.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.

data.title: RL.4.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.

data.title: RL.4.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.

data.title: RL.5.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.

data.title: RL.5.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 4–5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RL.5.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.

data.title: RL.5.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).

data.title: RL.5.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.

data.title: RL.5.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.

data.title: RL.5.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe how a narrator’s or speaker’s point of view influences how events are described.

data.title: RL.5.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or beauty of a text (e.g., graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, poem).

data.title: RL.5.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (e.g., mysteries and adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.

data.title: RL.6.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

data.title: RL.6.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

data.title: RL.6.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.

data.title: RL.6.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe how a particular story’s or drama’s plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.

data.title: RL.6.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.

data.title: RL.6.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.

data.title: RL.6.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.

data.title: RL.6.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they “see” and “hear” when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.

data.title: RL.6.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.

data.title: RL.7.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

data.title: RL.7.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

data.title: RL.7.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.

data.title: RL.7.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).

data.title: RL.7.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (e.g., alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.

data.title: RL.7.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how a drama’s or poem’s form or structure (e.g., soliloquy, sonnet) contributes to its meaning.

data.title: RL.7.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.

data.title: RL.7.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (e.g., lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film).

data.title: RL.7.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.

data.title: RL.8.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

data.title: RL.8.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RL.8.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to the characters, setting, and plot; provide an objective summary of the text.

data.title: RL.8.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a story or drama propel the action, reveal aspects of a character, or provoke a decision.

data.title: RL.8.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.

data.title: RL.8.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.

data.title: RL.8.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the audience or reader (e.g., created through the use of dramatic irony) create such effects as suspense or humor.

data.title: RL.8.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.

data.title: RL.8.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.

data.title: RL.9-10.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

data.title: RL.9-10.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 9–10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range. By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 9–10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RL.9-10.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze in detail its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.

data.title: RL.9-10.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how complex characters (e.g., those with multiple or conflicting motivations) develop over the course of a text, interact with other characters, and advance the plot or develop the theme.

data.title: RL.9-10.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language evokes a sense of time and place; how it sets a formal or informal tone).

data.title: RL.9-10.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how an author’s choices concerning how to structure a text, order events within it (e.g., parallel plots), and manipulate time (e.g., pacing, flashbacks) create such effects as mystery, tension, or surprise.

data.title: RL.9-10.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze a particular point of view or cultural experience reflected in a work of literature from outside the United States, drawing on a wide reading of world literature.

data.title: RL.9-10.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze the representation of a subject or a key scene in two different artistic mediums, including what is emphasized or absent in each treatment (e.g., Auden’s “Musée des Beaux Arts” and Breughel’s Landscape with the Fall of Icarus).

data.title: RL.9-10.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific work (e.g., how Shakespeare treats a theme or topic from Ovid or the Bible or how a later author draws on a play by Shakespeare).

data.title: RL.K.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.

data.title: RL.K.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.

data.title: RL.K.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.

data.title: RL.K.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story.

data.title: RL.K.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.

data.title: RL.K.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recognize common types of texts (e.g., storybooks, poems).

data.title: RL.K.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, name the author and illustrator of a story and define the role of each in telling the story.

data.title: RL.K.7
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts).

data.title: RL.K.9
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.

data.title: RST.11-12.1
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts, attending to important distinctions the author makes and to any gaps or inconsistencies in the account.

data.title: RST.11-12.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 11–CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RST.11-12.2
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; summarize complex concepts, processes, or information presented in a text by paraphrasing them in simpler but still accurate terms.

data.title: RST.11-12.3
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Follow precisely a complex multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks; analyze the specific results based on explanations in the text.

data.title: RST.11-12.4
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 11–12 texts and topics.

data.title: RST.11-12.5
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze how the text structures information or ideas into categories or hierarchies, demonstrating understanding of the information or ideas.

data.title: RST.11-12.6
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze the author’s purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text, identifying important issues that remain unresolved.

data.title: RST.11-12.7
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., quantitative data, video, multimedia) in order to address a question or solve a problem.

data.title: RST.11-12.8
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Evaluate the hypotheses, data, analysis, and conclusions in a science or technical text, verifying the data when possible and corroborating or challenging conclusions with other sources of information.

data.title: RST.11-12.9
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Synthesize information from a range of sources (e.g., texts, experiments, simulations) into a coherent understanding of a process, phenomenon, or concept, resolving conflicting information when possible.

data.title: RST.6-8.1
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.

data.title: RST.6-8.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RST.6-8.2
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.

data.title: RST.6-8.3
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.

data.title: RST.6-8.4
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6–8 texts and topics.

data.title: RST.6-8.5
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.

data.title: RST.6-8.6
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze the author’s purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.

data.title: RST.6-8.7
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).

data.title: RST.6-8.8
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.

data.title: RST.6-8.9
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.

data.title: RST.9-10.1
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts, attending to the precise details of explanations or descriptions.

data.title: RST.9-10.10
data.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 9–10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

data.title: RST.9-10.2
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; trace the text’s explanation or depiction of a complex process, phenomenon, or concept; provide an accurate summary of the text.

data.title: RST.9-10.3
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Follow precisely a complex multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks, attending to special cases or exceptions defined in the text.

data.title: RST.9-10.4
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 9–10 texts and topics.

data.title: RST.9-10.5
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze the structure of the relationships among concepts in a text, including relationships among key terms (e.g., force, friction, reaction force, energy).

data.title: RST.9-10.6
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Analyze the author’s purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text, defining the question the author seeks to address.

data.title: RST.9-10.7
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Translate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text into visual form (e.g., a table or chart) and translate information expressed visually or mathematically (e.g., in an equation) into words.

data.title: RST.9-10.8
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Assess the extent to which the reasoning and evidence in a text support the author’s claim or a recommendation for solving a scientific or technical problem.

data.title: RST.9-10.9
data.subject: Literacy in History/Social Studies, Science, and Technical Subjects
data.description: Compare and contrast findings presented in a text to those from other sources (including their own experiments), noting when the findings support or contradict previous explanations or accounts.

data.title: SL.1.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.

data.title: SL.1.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).

data.title: SL.1.1.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Build on others’ talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.

data.title: SL.1.1.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.

data.title: SL.1.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.

data.title: SL.1.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.

data.title: SL.1.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.

data.title: SL.1.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.

data.title: SL.1.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 1 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 26 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.2.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.

data.title: SL.2.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).

data.title: SL.2.1.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Build on others’ talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.

data.title: SL.2.1.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.

data.title: SL.2.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.

data.title: SL.2.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.

data.title: SL.2.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.

data.title: SL.2.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.

data.title: SL.2.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification. (See grade 2 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 26 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.3.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.

data.title: SL.3.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.

data.title: SL.3.1.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).

data.title: SL.3.1.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.

data.title: SL.3.1.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.

data.title: SL.3.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

data.title: SL.3.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.

data.title: SL.3.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.

data.title: SL.3.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.

data.title: SL.3.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification. (See grade 3 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 28 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.4.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.

data.title: SL.4.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.

data.title: SL.4.1.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.

data.title: SL.4.1.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.

data.title: SL.4.1.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.

data.title: SL.4.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

data.title: SL.4.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.

data.title: SL.4.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.

data.title: SL.4.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.

data.title: SL.4.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 4 Language standards 1 on page 28 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.5.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others’ ideas and expressing their own clearly.

data.title: SL.5.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.

data.title: SL.5.1.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.

data.title: SL.5.1.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.

data.title: SL.5.1.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.

data.title: SL.5.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

data.title: SL.5.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.

data.title: SL.5.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.

data.title: SL.5.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, sound) and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.

data.title: SL.5.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 5 Language standards 1 and 3 on page 28 for specific expectations.)

data.title: SL.K.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.

data.title: SL.K.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).

data.title: SL.K.1.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.

data.title: SL.K.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.

data.title: SL.K.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.

data.title: SL.K.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events and, with prompting and support, provide additional detail.

data.title: SL.K.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions as desired to provide additional detail.

data.title: SL.K.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.

data.title: W.1.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide some sense of closure.

data.title: W.11-12.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.

data.title: W.11-12.10
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

data.title: W.11-12.1.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Introduce precise, knowledgeable claim(s), establish the significance of the claim(s), distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and create an organization that logically sequences claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.

data.title: W.11-12.1.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Develop claim(s) and counterclaims fairly and thoroughly, supplying the most relevant evidence for each while pointing out the strengths and limitations of both in a manner that anticipates the audience’s knowledge level, concerns, values, and possible biases.

data.title: W.11-12.1.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use words, phrases, and clauses as well as varied syntax to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons, between reasons and evidence, and between claim(s) and counterclaims.

data.title: W.11-12.1.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.

data.title: W.11-12.1.e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.

data.title: W.11-12.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas, concepts, and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.

data.title: W.11-12.2.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Introduce a topic; organize complex ideas, concepts, and information so that each new element builds on that which precedes it to create a unified whole; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., figures, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.

data.title: W.11-12.2.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Develop the topic thoroughly by selecting the most significant and relevant facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples appropriate to the audience’s knowledge of the topic.

data.title: W.11-12.2.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use appropriate and varied transitions and syntax to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among complex ideas and concepts.

data.title: W.11-12.2.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use precise language, domain-specific vocabulary, and techniques such as metaphor, simile, and analogy to manage the complexity of the topic.

data.title: W.11-12.2.e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.

data.title: W.11-12.2.f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented (e.g., articulating implications or the significance of the topic).

data.title: W.11-12.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.

data.title: W.11-12.3.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Engage and orient the reader by setting out a problem, situation, or observation and its significance, establishing one or multiple point(s) of view, and introducing a narrator and/or characters; create a smooth progression of experiences or events.

data.title: W.11-12.3.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description, reflection, and multiple plot lines, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.

data.title: W.11-12.3.c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use a variety of techniques to sequence events so that they build on one another to create a coherent whole and build toward a particular tone and outcome (e.g., a sense of mystery, suspense, growth, or resolution).

data.title: W.11-12.3.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use precise words and phrases, telling details, and sensory language to convey a vivid picture of the experiences, events, setting, and/or characters.

data.title: W.11-12.3.e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on what is experienced, observed, or resolved over the course of the narrative.

data.title: W.11-12.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)

data.title: W.11-12.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grades 11–12 on page 55.)

data.title: W.11-12.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce, publish, and update individual or shared writing products in response to ongoing feedback, including new arguments or information.

data.title: W.11-12.7
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question) or solve a problem; narrow or broaden the inquiry when appropriate; synthesize multiple sources on the subject, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.

data.title: W.11-12.8
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative print and digital sources, using advanced searches effectively; assess the strengths and limitations of each source in terms of the task, purpose, and audience; integrate information into the text selectively to maintain the flow of ideas, avoiding plagiarism and overreliance on any one source and following a standard format for citation.

data.title: W.11-12.9
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.

data.title: W.11-12.9.a
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Apply grades 11–12 Reading standards to literature (e.g., “Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics”).

data.title: W.11-12.9.b
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Apply grades 11–12 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., “Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U.S. texts, including the application of constitutional principles and use of legal reasoning [e.g., in U.S. Supreme Court Case majority opinions and dissents] and the premises, purposes, and arguments in works of public advocacy [e.g., The Federalist, presidential addresses]”).

data.title: W.1.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure.

data.title: W.1.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide some sense of closure.

data.title: W.1.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.

data.title: W.1.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.

data.title: W.1.7
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of “how-to” books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).

data.title: W.1.8
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.

data.title: W.2.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words (e.g., because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section.

data.title: W.2.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.

data.title: W.2.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write narratives in which they recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events, include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide a sense of closure.

data.title: W.2.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.

data.title: W.2.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.

data.title: W.2.7
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations).

data.title: W.2.8
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.

data.title: W.3.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.

data.title: W.3.10
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.

data.title: W.3.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.

data.title: W.3.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.

data.title: W.3.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.

data.title: W.3.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a concluding statement or section.

data.title: W.3.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.

data.title: W.3.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.

data.title: W.3.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.

data.title: W.3.2c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.

data.title: W.3.2d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a concluding statement or section.

data.title: W.3.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.

data.title: W.3.3a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.

data.title: W.3.3b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.

data.title: W.3.3c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.

data.title: W.3.3d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a sense of closure.

data.title: W.3.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)

data.title: W.3.5
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 3 on page 29.)

data.title: W.3.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.

data.title: W.3.7
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.

data.title: W.3.8
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.

data.title: W.4.1
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.

data.title: W.4.10
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.

data.title: W.4.1a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer’s purpose.

data.title: W.4.1b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.

data.title: W.4.1c
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).

data.title: W.4.1d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.

data.title: W.4.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.

data.title: W.4.2a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.

data.title: W.4.2b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.

data.description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.

data.title: W.4.2e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.

data.title: W.4.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.

data.subject: English Language Arts

data.title: W.4.3b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.

data.title: W.4.3c
data.subject: English Language Arts

data.title: W.4.3d
data.subject: English Language Arts

data.title: W.4.3e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.

data.title: W.4.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 4 on page 29.)

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one page in a single sitting.

data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge

data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts

data.title: W.5.1

data.title: W.5.5

data.description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 5 on page 29.)

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of two pages in a single sitting.

data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge

data.title: W.6.1

data.title: W.6.1.b

data.description: Establish and maintain a formal style.

data.title: W.6.1.e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.

data.title: W.6.2
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.

data.title: W.6.2.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.

data.title: W.6.2.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.

data.title: W.6.2.c

data.description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.

data.title: W.6.2.d
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Establish and maintain a formal style.

data.title: W.6.2.f
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.

data.title: W.6.3
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.

data.title: W.6.3.a
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.

data.title: W.6.3.b
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.

data.title: W.6.3.c

data.title: W.6.3.e
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.

data.title: W.6.4
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach. data.title: W.6.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of three pages in a single sitting.

data.title: W.6.7

data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literature (e.g., “Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres [e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories] in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics”).

data.description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. data.description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. data.title: W.8.6
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.

data.title: W.8.7
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.

data.title: W.8.8
data.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
data.subject: English Language Arts
data.description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
